questionText: Você está em uma galeria de arte em Londres e quer expressar que uma pintura abstrata é extremamente perturbadora de maneira positiva, como faz um crítico nativo. Qual expressão você usaria?
correctAnswer: This piece is deeply unsettling in the most arresting way.
options: This piece is deeply unsettling in the most arresting way.
This painting is very strange and I don't understand it.
This artwork looks weird but somehow nice.
This picture makes me feel confused in a good manner.

questionText: Um amigo americano pergunta sua opinião sobre o último álbum de um artista. Você quer dizer que o álbum superou todas as suas expectativas de forma eloquente. O que você diz?
correctAnswer: It far exceeded my expectations — a genuinely revelatory body of work.
options: It far exceeded my expectations — a genuinely revelatory body of work.
The album was very good, better than I thought it would be.
I liked it more than I expected, it was really nice.
It surpassed what I was thinking would happen with the songs.

questionText: Você está em uma discussão sobre cinema e quer elogiar a direção de um filme dizendo que o diretor tem um olhar completamente singular. Como um nativo expressaria isso?
correctAnswer: The director brings an utterly distinctive cinematic vision to every frame.
options: The director brings an utterly distinctive cinematic vision to every frame.
The director has a very unique way of making movies that is special.
This director is different from others because he films in his own style.
The person who directed this movie has a really unusual eye for things.

questionText: Em uma resenha teatral, você deseja destacar que a performance de um ator foi absolutamente cativante do início ao fim. Qual seria a forma mais natural em inglês nativo?
correctAnswer: His performance was utterly riveting from the opening scene to the final curtain.
options: His performance was utterly riveting from the opening scene to the final curtain.
He acted very well in all parts of the play from start to finish.
His acting was good and kept my attention during the whole show.
The actor performed in a captivating manner throughout the entire performance.

questionText: Você quer recomendar um livro a um amigo dizendo que ele mudou completamente a sua perspectiva sobre o mundo. Como diria isso de forma sofisticada em inglês?
correctAnswer: This book fundamentally shifted the way I perceive the world around me.
options: This book fundamentally shifted the way I perceive the world around me.
This book changed my thoughts and now I see things in a new way.
Reading this book made me think differently about many different things.
After reading this book, my perspective about the world became different.

questionText: Em um podcast sobre música, o apresentador pede que você descreva o que torna o jazz de Miles Davis tão inovador. Qual resposta demonstra melhor fluência nativa?
correctAnswer: Davis had an uncanny ability to push boundaries while maintaining an unmistakable sense of musical identity.
options: Davis had an uncanny ability to push boundaries while maintaining an unmistakable sense of musical identity.
Miles Davis was very innovative because he played jazz in a new way that people hadn't heard before.
Davis was creative and different, and his style of jazz was unlike what anyone else was doing.
Miles Davis played music that was very original and had his own special sound that everyone recognized.

questionText: Um colega britânico pergunta se você assistiu à série nova que todo mundo está falando. Você quer dizer que ainda não viu, mas que está na sua lista. Como responderia naturalmente?
correctAnswer: I haven't got round to it yet, but it's definitely on my watchlist.
options: I haven't got round to it yet, but it's definitely on my watchlist.
No, I did not watch it still, but I will put it on my list to see.
I haven't seen it yet but I am planning to watch it sometime soon.
Not yet, I need to add it to the list of things I want to watch.

questionText: Você está descrevendo uma peça de teatro experimental para amigos e quer dizer que ela desafiou todas as convenções do teatro tradicional. Qual expressão é mais precisa?
correctAnswer: It threw conventional theatrical norms completely out of the window.
options: It threw conventional theatrical norms completely out of the window.
The play broke the normal rules of theater that everyone knows.
This theater show did not follow the traditional ways of making plays.
It was a play that challenged the conventions in a very radical way.

questionText: Em uma conversa casual, você quer dizer que um festival de cinema que visitou foi uma experiência inesquecível. Como expressaria isso de forma coloquial e nativa?
correctAnswer: It was an absolute blast — one of those experiences you just can't shake.
options: It was an absolute blast — one of those experiences you just can't shake.
The film festival was very memorable and I will never forget it ever.
Going to the festival was really nice and I remember it very well.
I had a great time at the cinema festival which was very unforgettable.

questionText: Você quer expressar discordância educada com a interpretação de um crítico sobre uma obra literária, sugerindo que ele perdeu nuances importantes. Como faria isso com elegância?
correctAnswer: I'd respectfully take issue with that reading — I think it overlooks some crucial subtleties.
options: I'd respectfully take issue with that reading — I think it overlooks some crucial subtleties.
I don't agree with the critic because I think he missed important things.
The critic's opinion is wrong because he didn't understand the book well.
I think the critic made mistakes in his analysis of the literary work.

questionText: Durante uma entrevista, um jornalista pede que você justifique por que a literatura do século XX é relevante hoje. Você quer argumentar que ela antecipou dilemas contemporâneos. Como diria?
correctAnswer: Twentieth-century literature anticipated many of the existential dilemmas we grapple with today.
options: Twentieth-century literature anticipated many of the existential dilemmas we grapple with today.
Books from the 1900s predicted problems that we are having in our lives now.
Literature from the last century talked about things that are still problems today.
The books written in the twentieth century are still relevant because they speak of modern issues.

questionText: Você assistiu a um concerto ao vivo e quer transmitir a energia arrepiante da performance para um amigo. Qual frase captura melhor esse sentimento?
correctAnswer: The energy in that venue was electrifying — it gave me goosebumps from start to finish.
options: The energy in that venue was electrifying — it gave me goosebumps from start to finish.
The concert had a lot of energy and I got goosebumps during the whole show.
It was a very energetic concert and I felt chills all over my body the entire time.
The show was very exciting and I had goosebumps all through the performance.

questionText: Em uma discussão literária, você quer dizer que o narrador não confiável de um romance é o que torna a obra fascinante. Como formularia isso com precisão?
correctAnswer: The unreliable narrator is precisely what gives the novel its unsettling brilliance.
options: The unreliable narrator is precisely what gives the novel its unsettling brilliance.
The narrator who is not reliable is what makes the book interesting and good.
Because the narrator lies, the novel becomes more exciting and fascinating for readers.
The book is great because the narrator doesn't always tell the truth which is interesting.

questionText: Você está em um debate sobre o impacto cultural do streaming e quer argumentar que ele democratizou o acesso à cultura. Como expressaria esse argumento com sofisticação?
correctAnswer: Streaming platforms have genuinely democratised access to cultural content on an unprecedented scale.
options: Streaming platforms have genuinely democratised access to cultural content on an unprecedented scale.
Streaming services made culture available to more people than it was before in history.
Now more people can access culture because of streaming and this is very good.
Streaming has allowed everyone to watch cultural things that were difficult to access before.

questionText: Você quer elogiar um documentário dizendo que ele lança uma nova luz sobre um tema que parecia estar completamente esgotado. Qual seria a forma mais elegante?
correctAnswer: It sheds fresh light on a subject that seemed to have been exhausted by prior treatments.
options: It sheds fresh light on a subject that seemed to have been exhausted by prior treatments.
The documentary shows new things about a topic that we already knew a lot about.
This documentary talks about an old subject in a new way that is interesting.
The documentary presents new information about a theme that many others covered before.

questionText: Em uma resenha de álbum, você quer dizer que as letras do artista são evocativas e introspectivas. Qual expressão soa mais natural para um falante nativo?
correctAnswer: The lyrics are hauntingly introspective, with an evocative quality that lingers long after the music ends.
options: The lyrics are hauntingly introspective, with an evocative quality that lingers long after the music ends.
The song words are thoughtful and make you think about life after the song is over.
The lyrics of the songs are deep and emotional and stay in your mind for a long time.
The artist writes very introspective lyrics that are evocative and you remember them.

questionText: Você quer descrever a atmosfera de um museu de arte contemporânea dizendo que o espaço em si faz parte da experiência artística. Como diria com naturalidade?
correctAnswer: The space itself is very much part of the curatorial experience — architecture and art in dialogue.
options: The space itself is very much part of the curatorial experience — architecture and art in dialogue.
The building of the museum is also important for the art experience inside it.
In this museum, the space where the art is shown is also part of the art.
The museum building and the art inside it work together to create an experience.

questionText: Numa discussão sobre adaptações cinematográficas, você quer argumentar que o filme traiu o espírito do livro original. Como formularia isso com precisão crítica?
correctAnswer: The film fundamentally betrayed the spirit of the source material, stripping it of its thematic depth.
options: The film fundamentally betrayed the spirit of the source material, stripping it of its thematic depth.
The movie did not respect the book well and lost many important things from the story.
The film version was not faithful to the book and missed the main themes of the original.
The movie betrayed the original book by not keeping the important elements of the story.

questionText: Em uma conversa sobre arquitetura urbana, você quer expressar que um edifício histórico deveria ser preservado a todo custo como parte do patrimônio cultural. Como diria com autoridade?
correctAnswer: That building is an irreplaceable piece of our architectural heritage and must be preserved at all costs.
options: That building is an irreplaceable piece of our architectural heritage and must be preserved at all costs.
We should keep the old building because it is part of our culture and history.
The historic building is important for our heritage so it shouldn't be destroyed.
This old building is a cultural landmark that we need to protect from being demolished.

questionText: Você está analisando uma obra de arte performática e quer questionar se ela realmente transcende os limites entre arte e vida cotidiana. Como formularia essa reflexão criticamente?
correctAnswer: One has to ask whether this work genuinely dissolves the boundary between art and lived experience, or merely gestures towards it.
options: One has to ask whether this work genuinely dissolves the boundary between art and lived experience, or merely gestures towards it.
We need to question if this performance art is really mixing art with everyday life or just trying to.
The question is whether this artwork truly breaks the line between art and normal life.
This performance makes us think about whether art and life can be the same thing or not.

questionText: Durante um painel literário, você quer sugerir que o realismo mágico de García Márquez subverte as expectativas do leitor ocidental de maneira deliberada. Como articularia isso?
correctAnswer: García Márquez deliberately subverts Western narrative expectations through his deployment of magical realism.
options: García Márquez deliberately subverts Western narrative expectations through his deployment of magical realism.
García Márquez uses magical realism in his books to surprise readers from Western countries.
The author intentionally writes magical realism to challenge how Western readers think about stories.
García Márquez writes magical realist stories that are meant to confuse Western readers on purpose.

questionText: Você quer elogiar a trilha sonora de um filme dizendo que ela amplifica a tensão emocional de cada cena sem nunca se tornar intrusiva. Qual seria a forma mais sofisticada?
correctAnswer: The score amplifies the emotional tension of every scene without ever becoming heavy-handed.
options: The score amplifies the emotional tension of every scene without ever becoming heavy-handed.
The movie music makes each scene more emotional and tense but is not too much or too loud.
The film's soundtrack helps every scene feel more emotional without being too obvious or intrusive.
The music in the film increases the emotion of each scene while remaining subtle and not excessive.

questionText: Em uma entrevista sobre curadoria de exposições, você quer dizer que o objetivo era criar um diálogo entre obras de diferentes períodos históricos. Como expressaria isso com precisão curatorial?
correctAnswer: The curatorial intent was to establish a conversation between works spanning vastly different historical periods.
options: The curatorial intent was to establish a conversation between works spanning vastly different historical periods.
We wanted the artworks from different time periods to speak to each other in the exhibition.
The goal of the exhibition was to put together art from various historical periods to talk to each other.
We tried to create a dialogue by placing art works from very different periods of history together.

questionText: Você está discutindo o impacto de um romance distópico e quer argumentar que sua relevância cresceu com o tempo, não diminuiu. Como construiria esse argumento?
correctAnswer: Far from losing its relevance, this dystopian novel has only grown more prescient with the passing decades.
options: Far from losing its relevance, this dystopian novel has only grown more prescient with the passing decades.
This dystopian novel is still relevant today and has become even more important over the years.
The relevance of this dystopian book has not decreased but has actually increased over time.
This dystopian novel did not become less relevant with time; instead it became more relevant.

questionText: Em uma crítica teatral, você quer descrever uma encenação minimalista que, paradoxalmente, criou um impacto visual imenso. Como capturaria essa contradição elegantemente?
correctAnswer: The production's radical minimalism paradoxically produced a visual impact of staggering proportions.
options: The production's radical minimalism paradoxically produced a visual impact of staggering proportions.
The play had very few elements on stage but somehow it created a very big visual effect.
Despite being minimalist, this theater production had a surprisingly large visual impact on the audience.
The minimalist approach of the show paradoxically resulted in a very impressive visual experience.

questionText: Você está defendendo a preservação da música folclórica regional como forma de resistência cultural. Como articularia essa posição com convicção intelectual?
correctAnswer: Regional folk music represents an act of cultural resistance — a living repository of collective memory that must not be allowed to perish.
options: Regional folk music represents an act of cultural resistance — a living repository of collective memory that must not be allowed to perish.
Folk music from different regions is important for culture and memory and we should not let it die.
Regional folk music is a form of cultural resistance because it keeps the memory of communities alive.
We should preserve folk music from different regions because it is an important part of cultural heritage.

questionText: Você quer expressar que um romance policial subverte o gênero ao rejeitar a resolução conveniente no final. Como diria isso com autoridade crítica?
correctAnswer: The novel subverts genre conventions by deliberately withholding the tidy resolution readers expect.
options: The novel subverts genre conventions by deliberately withholding the tidy resolution readers expect.
This crime novel is different because it doesn't have the expected ending that readers usually want.
The mystery book breaks the rules of the genre by not giving readers the resolution they are waiting for.
This novel is unusual for a crime story because it purposely refuses to give a satisfying conclusion.

questionText: Em uma conversa com um curador, você quer questionar se a arte digital pode ter o mesmo peso emocional que obras físicas tradicionais. Como levantaria essa questão com sofisticação?
correctAnswer: Can digital art ever carry the same visceral emotional weight as a physically present, materially grounded work?
options: Can digital art ever carry the same visceral emotional weight as a physically present, materially grounded work?
Is it possible for digital art to make people feel as emotional as real physical artworks do?
Do you think digital art can have the same emotional impact as traditional physical art pieces?
Can art made with computers create the same feelings in people as paintings or sculptures do?

questionText: Você quer descrever a carreira de um ator dizendo que ele nunca se repetiu e sempre buscou papéis que desafiassem sua própria técnica. Como diria com admiração genuína?
correctAnswer: He has consistently refused to rest on his laurels, always seeking roles that push his craft to its limits.
options: He has consistently refused to rest on his laurels, always seeking roles that push his craft to its limits.
The actor never did the same type of role twice and always tried to find challenging parts to play.
He has always looked for difficult roles that challenge him rather than repeating what he already did well.
This actor never repeated himself and was always searching for roles that would test his acting abilities.

questionText: Durante um debate sobre música popular, você quer argumentar que a comercialização da música a esvaziou de significado artístico genuíno. Como construiria esse argumento com precisão?
correctAnswer: The relentless commercialisation of popular music has progressively hollowed out its artistic integrity.
options: The relentless commercialisation of popular music has progressively hollowed out its artistic integrity.
Popular music has lost its artistic value because the music industry is focused only on making money.
Because music has become too commercial, it has gradually lost its genuine artistic meaning.
The commercialization of the music industry has made popular music less artistically significant over time.

questionText: Você está analisando o trabalho de um fotógrafo documental e quer dizer que suas imagens capturam a condição humana em sua vulnerabilidade mais essencial. Como expressaria isso?
correctAnswer: His photographs capture the human condition at its most raw and unguarded.
options: His photographs capture the human condition at its most raw and unguarded.
The photographer's pictures show human beings in their most vulnerable and real moments.
His photos take pictures of people in vulnerable situations that show what being human is like.
The pictures capture humanity in moments of vulnerability that reveal the real human condition.

questionText: Em uma conversa acadêmica sobre Shakespeare, você quer argumentar que suas obras continuam sendo interpretadas de novas maneiras porque são intrinsecamente abertas. Como diria?
correctAnswer: Shakespeare's plays endure precisely because their thematic openness invites perpetual reinterpretation across every era.
options: Shakespeare's plays endure precisely because their thematic openness invites perpetual reinterpretation across every era.
Shakespeare's works last because they can be understood in many different ways in every time period.
The plays of Shakespeare are always relevant because they allow for different interpretations throughout history.
Shakespeare wrote plays that can be interpreted differently by every generation, which is why they last.

questionText: Você está escrevendo sobre um escritor cujo estilo desafia categorização e quer dizer que ele defy qualquer tentativa de encaixá-lo em um movimento literário. Como escreveria isso?
correctAnswer: His prose stubbornly resists any attempt to pin it down within a recognisable literary movement.
options: His prose stubbornly resists any attempt to pin it down within a recognisable literary movement.
The writer's style is so unique that it cannot be placed in any specific literary movement or category.
It is impossible to classify this author's writing style within any traditional literary movement.
This writer refuses to be categorized because his style of writing does not fit any literary movement.

questionText: Você quer argumentar, em um ensaio, que a censura artística revela mais sobre os medos de quem censura do que sobre a obra censurada. Como formularia essa tese com eloquência?
correctAnswer: Artistic censorship invariably tells us more about the insecurities of those who impose it than about the work it seeks to suppress.
options: Artistic censorship invariably tells us more about the insecurities of those who impose it than about the work it seeks to suppress.
When art is censored, we learn more about why the people who do the censoring are afraid than about the art itself.
Censoring art reveals the fears of those who censor more than it says anything about the censored work.
Art censorship shows us more about the mentality of those who apply it than about the artworks they ban.

questionText: Em uma masterclass de composição musical, você quer dizer que a dissonância não deve ser evitada, mas abraçada como ferramenta expressiva. Como articularia esse princípio?
correctAnswer: Dissonance ought not to be resolved out of compositional timidity but embraced as an expressive tool in its own right.
options: Dissonance ought not to be resolved out of compositional timidity but embraced as an expressive tool in its own right.
We should not be afraid of dissonance in music but use it as a way to express things in composition.
Dissonance should not be avoided in music because it is an important tool for expressing emotions.
Composers should embrace dissonance rather than avoiding it since it can be used as an expressive element.

questionText: Você quer contestar, com sofisticação filosófica, a afirmação de que a arte deve necessariamente ser politicamente engajada. Como construiria esse contra-argumento?
correctAnswer: To insist that all art must be politically engaged is to impoverish the aesthetic experience by subjugating it to ideology.
options: To insist that all art must be politically engaged is to impoverish the aesthetic experience by subjugating it to ideology.
Not all art needs to be political because when art has to be political it loses its aesthetic value.
Art doesn't need to be politically engaged because forcing it to be reduces its artistic and aesthetic qualities.
The idea that all art must engage with politics limits art and reduces the richness of aesthetic experience.

questionText: Em uma conferência sobre cinema de arte, você quer argumentar que a lentidão narrativa de Tarkovsky não é um defeito, mas a própria essência de sua poética. Como diria isso com profundidade?
correctAnswer: Tarkovsky's unhurried pace is not a formal deficiency but the very medium through which his metaphysical inquiry unfolds.
options: Tarkovsky's unhurried pace is not a formal deficiency but the very medium through which his metaphysical inquiry unfolds.
Tarkovsky's slow films are not bad but they are slow on purpose because he is exploring philosophical ideas.
The slowness in Tarkovsky's movies is intentional and is how he expresses his deep philosophical thoughts.
Tarkovsky's narrative pace is deliberately slow because it is the way he communicates his philosophical vision.

questionText: Você está debatendo se obras de arte pertencentes a museus europeus deveriam ser repatriadas para seus países de origem. Quer argumentar que a repatriação é imperativa moralmente. Como?
correctAnswer: The moral imperative for repatriation is unambiguous: these objects were acquired under conditions of colonial coercion and belong to the cultures that created them.
options: The moral imperative for repatriation is unambiguous: these objects were acquired under conditions of colonial coercion and belong to the cultures that created them.
Art should be returned to its country of origin because it was taken during colonial times and doesn't belong to European museums.
Repatriation is morally necessary since these artworks were stolen from their original cultures during colonialism.
The moral argument for returning art is clear because these objects were taken by force during colonial periods.

questionText: Em um ensaio sobre pós-modernismo literário, você quer dizer que a metaficção expõe o caráter artificial de toda narrativa. Como formularia essa tese com precisão teórica?
correctAnswer: Metafiction lays bare the constructedness of all narrative, reminding the reader that fiction is never a transparent window onto reality.
options: Metafiction lays bare the constructedness of all narrative, reminding the reader that fiction is never a transparent window onto reality.
Metafiction shows that all stories are made up and reminds readers that fiction is not a direct view of reality.
When fiction reflects on itself, it reveals that all narratives are artificial and not direct representations of reality.
Metafiction demonstrates that fiction is constructed and tells readers that stories are not real windows to reality.

questionText: Você quer descrever a tensão entre o artista e seu público quando a obra deliberadamente aliena quem a contempla. Como capturaria esse fenômeno criticamente?
correctAnswer: There is a productive tension at work when the artwork wilfully alienates its audience, forcing them into active interpretive labour.
options: There is a productive tension at work when the artwork wilfully alienates its audience, forcing them into active interpretive labour.
When art purposely confuses its audience, it creates a tension that makes viewers work harder to understand it.
Art that deliberately alienates the audience creates a productive conflict that requires active interpretation.
The tension between artist and audience is productive when the work is designed to challenge and alienate viewers.

questionText: Em uma análise de ópera, você quer argumentar que a voz de Maria Callas transcendeu a técnica e atingiu uma dimensão quase espiritual. Como expressaria essa ideia?
correctAnswer: Callas transcended mere technical mastery; her voice seemed to carry an almost numinous spiritual dimension.
options: Callas transcended mere technical mastery; her voice seemed to carry an almost numinous spiritual dimension.
Maria Callas was more than just a technically skilled singer because her voice had a spiritual quality to it.
Callas's voice went beyond technique and had a kind of spiritual power that made her singing transcendent.
The singing of Maria Callas surpassed technical skill and her voice had a deeply spiritual and transcendent quality.

questionText: Você está argumentando que o kitsch não é necessariamente inferior à alta arte, mas opera em um register estético diferente. Como formularia essa posição teórica?
correctAnswer: Kitsch need not be dismissed as aesthetically inferior; it operates within a distinct register that serves its own legitimate cultural functions.
options: Kitsch need not be dismissed as aesthetically inferior; it operates within a distinct register that serves its own legitimate cultural functions.
Kitsch is not necessarily worse than high art because it has its own aesthetic category and cultural purposes.
We should not consider kitsch to be lower quality than high art since it fulfills different cultural functions.
Kitsch art is not necessarily of lower quality than high art because it serves different aesthetic and cultural purposes.

questionText: Em uma palestra sobre improvisação no jazz, você quer explicar que a liberdade do improvisador é paradoxalmente estruturada pela gramática musical que ele internalizou. Como diria?
correctAnswer: The jazz improviser's freedom is paradoxically grounded in a deeply internalised musical grammar that simultaneously enables and constrains.
options: The jazz improviser's freedom is paradoxically grounded in a deeply internalised musical grammar that enables and constrains.
Jazz improvisers are free to play what they want but they are also limited by the musical rules they have learned.
Jazz improvisation is both free and structured because musicians use musical rules they have internalized deeply.
When improvising in jazz, musicians are free but also constrained by the musical grammar they have learned over years.

questionText: Você quer argumentar que a tradução literária é inevitavelmente um ato de criação e não apenas de transposição. Como formularia essa tese de forma elegante?
correctAnswer: Literary translation is inescapably an act of creative reinvention rather than mere transposition from one linguistic code to another.
options: Literary translation is inescapably an act of creative reinvention rather than mere transposition from one linguistic code to another.
Translating literature always involves creativity because it is not simply moving words from one language to another.
A literary translator cannot avoid being creative because translation is more than transferring meaning between languages.
Translating literary works is always a creative process and not just the mechanical transfer of words from one language to another.

questionText: Em uma análise de dança contemporânea, você quer dizer que o corpo do dançarino não é apenas um instrumento, mas o próprio texto da performance. Como articularia isso?
correctAnswer: In contemporary dance, the dancer's body is not merely an instrument of expression but the very text being written in real time.
options: In contemporary dance, the dancer's body is not merely an instrument of expression but the very text being written in real time.
In modern dance, the dancer's body is not just used for expression but is actually the message of the performance.
Contemporary dance uses the dancer's body not only as a tool but as the actual content and text of the performance.
The dancer's body in contemporary dance serves not just as an expressive instrument but as the work of art itself.

questionText: Você quer desafiar a premissa de que obras de arte clássicas são universais, argumentando que essa universalidade é uma construção ideológica. Como faria isso com rigor teórico?
correctAnswer: The supposed universality of the classical canon is itself an ideological construct that reflects the cultural authority of dominant groups.
options: The supposed universality of the classical canon is itself an ideological construct that reflects the cultural authority of dominant groups.
The idea that classical art is universal is actually a cultural and ideological construction made by powerful groups.
Classical art being called universal is an ideological idea that was created to justify the power of certain cultural groups.
The universality we attribute to classical works is actually an ideological construction imposed by culturally dominant groups.

questionText: Em uma análise sobre o impacto das redes sociais na crítica cultural, você quer argumentar que a democratização da opinião também trouxe a tirania do gosto médio. Como formularia isso?
correctAnswer: The democratisation of cultural opinion online has, paradoxically, given rise to a new tyranny of the median taste.
options: The democratisation of cultural opinion online has, paradoxically, given rise to a new tyranny of the median taste.
Social media has made cultural opinion more democratic but has also created a pressure to conform to average tastes.
While social media has democratized culture, it has paradoxically imposed a conformity to mainstream tastes on everyone.
The opening of cultural opinion to everyone on the internet has paradoxically created pressure toward average and mainstream taste.

questionText: Você está debatendo se a inteligência artificial pode produzir arte genuína e quer argumentar que arte requer intencionalidade e consciência, coisas que a IA não possui. Como diria?
correctAnswer: Genuine art presupposes intentionality and conscious experience; without these, what AI produces may be aesthetically compelling but remains fundamentally mimicry.
options: Genuine art presupposes intentionality and conscious experience; without these, what AI produces may be aesthetically compelling but remains fundamentally mimicry.
Real art requires intention and consciousness which AI doesn't have, so AI can only imitate art but not really create it.
Artificial intelligence cannot make genuine art because art needs intention and consciousness and AI has neither.
For something to be true art, it needs intentionality and consciousness, neither of which AI has, so AI cannot make real art.

questionText: Em uma conferência sobre memória cultural, você quer dizer que os monumentos não preservam a história, mas constroem narrativas seletivas sobre ela. Como articularia isso?
correctAnswer: Monuments do not preserve history so much as they construct selective narratives about it, encoding the values of those who erect them.
options: Monuments do not preserve history so much as they construct selective narratives about it, encoding the values of those who erect them.
Monuments don't just preserve history but create specific versions of it that reflect the values of who built them.
Rather than preserving history, monuments tell selective stories about it that reveal the values of those who created them.
Monuments construct narratives about history rather than preserving it, and those narratives reflect the builders' values.
